title: lobelia hilaireana
intro: Lobelia hilaireana, also known as Indian tobacco or pukeweed, is a perennial flowering plant native to Mexico and the southwestern United States. It has been traditionally used in medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include expectorant, anti-inflammatory, and antispasmodic properties. The health benefits of Lobelia hilaireana include its ability to relieve respiratory issues such as bronchitis, asthma, and coughs, as well as its potential to reduce anxiety and stress. Lobelia hilaireana can be prepared as a tea or tincture, and it is also used in herbal smoking blends, although its use as a smoking herb is not recommended due to potential toxicity.
